Package org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs

Class JcsSerializer

java.lang.Object
org.apache.juneau.marshall.Context
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingContextable
org.apache.juneau.marshall.MarshallingTraverseContext
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.Serializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.serializer.WriterSerializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.json.JsonSerializer
org.apache.juneau.marshall.jcs.JcsSerializer
All Implemented Interfaces:
JsonMetaProvider, ArrayRecordWritable, RecordWritable, TokenWritable
public class JcsSerializer extends JsonSerializer
Serializes POJO models to canonical JSON per RFC 8785 (JCS).
Media types

Produces media type: application/jcs+json

Description

JCS (JSON Canonicalization Scheme) produces a deterministic, byte-for-byte canonical representation of JSON, enabling reliable cryptographic operations such as hashing and digital signing. All canonicalization rules are defined in RFC 8785.

  • No whitespace between tokens.
  • Object properties sorted by UTF-16 code unit order, applied recursively to all nested objects.
  • Numbers serialized using ECMAScript-compatible rules: shortest round-trip representation, no trailing zeros, lowercase e, positive exponent sign included (e.g. 1e+30).
  • Negative zero serialized as 0.
  • Non-ASCII characters emitted as literal UTF-8 bytes (no unnecessary unicode escape sequences).
  • Array element order is preserved (not sorted).
Example:

// Create a bean. MyBean bean = new MyBean().name("Alice").age(30); // Serialize to canonical JSON. String json = JcsSerializer.DEFAULT.serialize(bean); // Or use the Jcs marshaller convenience method. String json = Jcs.of(bean);

Example output (bean with name/age):

{"age":30,"name":"Alice"}

Example output with nested object:

{"address":{"city":"Denver","zip":"80201"},"name":"Alice"}

Notes:
  • This class is thread safe and reusable.
  • JCS output is valid JSON and can be parsed using the standard JsonParser. The Jcs marshaller pairs this serializer with JsonParser for full round-trip support.
  • BigDecimal and BigInteger values beyond IEEE 754 double precision range will lose precision or throw a SerializeException. This is a spec-level constraint defined by RFC 8785.
  • Double.NaN, Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, and Double.NEGATIVE_INFINITY are not permitted and will throw a SerializeException.
  • Strings containing lone UTF-16 surrogate code units will throw SerializeException.
  • Uses application/jcs+json to avoid content-negotiation collision with JsonSerializer (application/json). Request explicitly via Accept: application/jcs+json for canonical output.
See Also:
  • RFC 8785 — JSON Canonicalization Scheme
  • JCS topic
  • Jcs
  • JsonParser

      Builders are used to define new contexts (e.g. serializers, parsers) based on existing configurations.

      Returns:
      A new Builder object.

    • createSession

      public JcsSerializerSession.Builder createSession()
      Description copied from class: Context
      Create a session builder based on the properties defined on this context.

      Use this method for creating sessions where you want to override basic settings. Otherwise, use Context.getSession() directly.

      Overrides:
      createSession in class JsonSerializer
      Returns:
      A new session builder.

    • serializeTokens

      public TokenWriter serializeTokens(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator (default session args) for the raw-JSON token writer. Real impl on JcsSerializerSession.serializeTokens(Object).

      Note: the cursor's structural methods (startObject, fieldName, string, ...) produce ordinary JSON; they do not apply the JCS canonicalization rules (sorted keys, normalized number formatting, etc.) that WriterSerializer.serialize(Object) produces. The object(Object) bridge is disabled on this writer. Use WriterSerializer.serialize(Object) for canonical output.

      Specified by:
      serializeTokens in interface TokenWritable
      Overrides:
      serializeTokens in class JsonSerializer
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new JsonTokenWriter with object(...) disabled.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • serializeRecords

      public RecordWriter serializeRecords(Object output) throws IOException
      Convenience delegator (default session args) for a record writer that emits canonical JCS for the value passed to write(...). Real impl on JcsSerializerSession.serializeRecords(Object).
      Parameters:
      output - The output.
      Returns:
      A new RecordWriter.
      Throws:
      IOException - If the output type is not supported or could not be opened.

    • isRecordStreaming

      public boolean isRecordStreaming()
      Description copied from interface: RecordWritable
      Streaming-quality signal queryable before opening a cursor.
      Returns:
      true if RecordWritable.serializeRecords(Object) returns an O(1)-memory streaming cursor; false for buffered (whole-value) formats backed by RecordAdapter.
